questions.questionText: Describe fully the locus of all points that are equidistant from two fixed points A and B.
questions.correctAnswer: The perpendicular bisector of the line segment AB

markScheme.desc: Passes through the midpoint of AB

workingSteps: Any point equidistant from A and B lies on the perpendicular bisector of AB.
This is a straight line passing through the midpoint of AB at right angles to AB.
questions.explanation: The locus of points equidistant from two points is the perpendicular bisector of the segment joining them.

questions.questionText:
The bearing of B from A is 065°.
Find the bearing of A from B.
workingSteps: The reverse bearing is found by adding (or subtracting) 180°.
questions.explanation: Back bearing = forward bearing ± 180°. Since 065 + 180 = 245 < 360, add 180.

questions.context: A circle has centre O and radius 10 cm. Chord AB = 16 cm. The perpendicular from O to AB meets AB at M.

parts.label: a
parts.questionText: Explain why M is the midpoint of AB.
parts.correctAnswer: The perpendicular from the centre to a chord bisects the chord.

markScheme.desc: The perpendicular from the centre bisects the chord oe

workingSteps: Circle theorem: the perpendicular from the centre of a circle to a chord bisects the chord.

parts.label: c
parts.questionText:
A point P on AB satisfies AP = 4 cm.
Calculate the length OP.
workingSteps: M is the midpoint of AB, so AM = 8 cm

parts.label: d
parts.questionText: State whether P is inside or outside the circle. Give a reason.
workingSteps: OP ≈ 7.21 cm < 10 cm (radius), so P is inside the circle.
